Name: curvature_aware_dynamic_precision_routing
Title: Dynamic Precision Routing Utilizing Curvature-Aware Metrics for Efficient Model Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a dynamic routing mechanism based on curvature-aware metrics can enhance inference efficiency in neural networks by allocating computational resources in real-time according to the geometric properties of input data distributions, contrasting with static mixed-precision approaches.
Related Work: Existing works like Spectral Latent Steering and Spectral Quantization Routing focus on optimizing inference through static or heuristic methods. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware diagnostics to dynamically allocate precision based on the real-time geometric properties of the activation landscape, providing an adaptive solution rather than a fixed one.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel framework for dynamic precision routing in neural network inference, leveraging curvature-aware metrics to enhance computational efficiency. Existing mixed-precision approaches often rely on static profiling, failing to adapt to the variability in input data distributions. We propose a mechanism that computes curvature diagnostics in real-time, allowing for the allocation of numerical precision based on the local geometry of the input. By incorporating curvature information, we aim to optimize the throughput-accuracy trade-off, ensuring that tokens in well-conditioned subspaces can leverage lower precision without sacrificing performance. This innovation will be evaluated across various benchmarks, with theoretical grounding provided through PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our method promises to maintain strict computational budgets while dynamically adapting to changing data landscapes, offering a transparent and reproducible solution to enhance the scalability of inference in real-world applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement the dynamic precision routing mechanism in a baseline neural network architecture.
2. Evaluate the performance on standard datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) by comparing accuracy and latency against static precision methods.
3. Measure the local curvature diagnostics during inference and analyze their impact on precision allocation.
4. Conduct ablation studies to assess the contribution of curvature-aware metrics to overall model performance.
5. Utilize throughput-accuracy Pareto fronts to benchmark against existing dynamic precision methods.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the computational overhead associated with real-time curvature diagnostics and the challenge of ensuring stable precision switching. Limitations may arise from the specific architectures used and their responsiveness to dynamic routing, as well as the generalizability of results across different types of neural networks.
